content: Language education's explicit teaching of 'reflexion' as a learnable metacognitive vocabulary and practice can be adapted to improve LLM self-reflection mechanisms, by fine-tuning LLMs to explicitly name and structure different stages of their own reflexive reasoning the same way we teach high school students to formalize reflective writing. This creates a grounded, human-aligned framework for LLM self-reflection that is more interpretable than current black-box multi-perspective reflection techniques.
